gdp_real_growth_pct.trend_reasoning: US GDP stable but facing cross-currents. Energy self-sufficiency provides buffer, but Iran war costs, Section 301 trade escalation against 76 trading partners, and oil shock consumer impact create headwinds. February CPI benign but forward-looking indicators deteriorating. 5 recession warning signs flagged.

inflation_cpi_pct.trend_reasoning: US February CPI steady at 2.4% but pre-dates Iran war oil shock. Gas prices at $3.63/gal (22-month high). March/April readings will capture $100+ oil pass-through. IEA 400M barrel SPR release provides partial but insufficient offset.

current_account_pct_gdp.trend_reasoning: US current account roughly stable. Energy self-sufficiency buffers oil shock. Section 301 investigations (16 overcapacity + 60 forced labor) could reduce imports medium-term. War costs add to transfers. Safe-haven dollar inflows support.

govt_debt_pct_gdp.trend_reasoning: US debt rising from war spending (Operation Epic Fury) entering third week. Senate failed to pass war powers resolution — no legislative check on spending. $175B tariff refund SCOTUS liability. GOP House majority razor-thin at 217-214 after Kiley exit. Defense supplementals inevitable.

sovereign_credit_rating.value: S&P: AA+, Moody's: Aa1, Fitch: AAA

central_bank_policy_rate_pct.trend_reasoning: Fed on hold at 3.625%. February CPI steady at 2.4% pre-war but March/April readings will capture oil shock. Central bank super week March 16-19 — stagflation dilemma limits rate cuts. War uncertainty keeps Fed cautious.

sovereign_bond_yield_10yr_pct.trend_reasoning: US 10yr yields rising on war spending, inflation expectations from oil shock, and fiscal uncertainty from $175B tariff refund liability. Bond market stress from leveraged basis trade ($1T+) echoes March 2020.

fdi_inflows_usd.trend_reasoning: US FDI inflows broadly stable. War creating uncertainty but safe-haven status supports. SCOTUS tariff ruling creates trade policy uncertainty. Defense sector attracting investment.

exchange_rate_vs_usd.trend_reasoning: USD strengthening as safe-haven during Middle East war. DXY at multi-month highs. War and global risk-off supporting dollar demand.

military_expenditure_pct_gdp.trend_reasoning: US defense spending growing from Iran war (Operation Epic Fury) now entering third week. Freedom Shield exercise in Korea with 18,000 troops. Shield of Americas alliance with 15 Latin American nations. SIPRI: global arms transfers +9.2%, US exports +27%.

value.note: First time European share exceeded Middle East. NATO European members: USA supplied 58% of their imports.

composite_national_power_index.trend_reasoning: US national power stable but stretched across multiple theaters. Iran war entering third week. Freedom Shield exercise in Korea while DPRK fires 10 missiles. Shield of Americas alliance launched with 15 Latin American nations. Section 301 investigations demonstrate trade leverage.

political_risk_premium_bps.methodology: Sovereign rating implied spread + adjustments for instability, conflict, sanctions, autocracy

overall_investment_risk_score.trend_reasoning: US investment risk rising from war costs, bond market stress, Section 301 trade escalation, and political fragility (217-214 House majority). Senate failed to constrain war spending. 5 recession warning signs. But institutional strength provides floor.

resource_self_sufficiency_index.methodology: Average of min(production/consumption, 1.0) for energy, food, water, minerals

supply_chain_chokepoint_exposure.methodology: 0.6 * maritime_chokepoint_dependency + 0.4 * normalized_trade_openness; elevated due to active Hormuz/Suez disruption

market_accessibility_score.methodology: Average of capital_account_openness, stock_market_cap/200, financial_dev_index, ease_of_business/100

trade_openness_trend.reasoning: United States trade openness broadly stable. Global trade disruption from Hormuz primarily affects Gulf-dependent routes. Some trade rerouting benefits alternative routes.

exchange_rate_vs_usd.reasoning: United States currency broadly stable against USD. No direct conflict exposure. Global risk-off sentiment creates mild depreciation pressure against safe-haven dollar.

top_partner_relationship_health.reasoning: US bilateral relationships strained across multiple fronts. EU split on Iran war support. Trump trade tensions with Canada, China. Russia intelligence-sharing concerns. But G7 coordinating on SPR release shows cooperation.

political_stability_trend.reasoning: US political stability declining. War with Iran has no congressional authorization — war powers vote failed. GOP House majority razor-thin at 217-214 after Kiley exit to independent. DHS Secretary fired. $175B tariff refund SCOTUS crisis. Rand Paul warns of 'disastrous' midterms.

active_flags.reason: Absolute change 6.8892 exceeds 3× typical (2.0) = 6.0; Event context: USA has active event triggers

narrative.executive_summary: The US-Iran war enters its third week with 6,000+ targets struck and no ceasefire in sight. GDP growth holds at 2.8% (stable) but recession warning signs multiply as gas prices hit $3.63/gal (22-month high). Government debt trajectory is accelerating with war spending unchecked after the Senate failed to pass a war powers resolution. The GOP House majority narrowed to 217-214 after Rep. Kiley's exit to independent. USTR launched dual Section 301 investigations targeting 16 economies for overcapacity and 60 countries for forced labor, representing the broadest trade escalation in decades.
key_changes_this_week: US-Iran war enters third week — 6,000+ targets struck, 1,348 Iranian civilians killed, 6 US troops killed in combat
IEA approves historic 400M barrel SPR release as oil stays above $100/bbl
USTR launches Section 301 investigations: overcapacity (16 economies, 21 sectors) + forced labor (60 countries)
Senate war powers vote fails — Trump authorized to continue Iran strikes without congressional constraint
Rep. Kiley leaves GOP for independent — House majority narrows to 217-214
BlackRock freezes billionaire cash-outs from $26B fund amid market turmoil
Trump launches Shield of Americas alliance with 15 Latin American nations
narrative.outlook: Near-term outlook dominated by Iran war duration and oil shock pass-through. February CPI steady at 2.4% but March/April readings will capture energy price surge. Fed faces stagflation dilemma at March 16-19 central bank super week. Bond market stress from leveraged basis trade ($1T+) echoes March 2020 dynamics. Political fragility with razor-thin House majority constrains fiscal response.
narrative.investor_implications: Defensive positioning warranted. Energy independence (score: 1.000) provides relative buffer but war costs and trade escalation create cross-currents. Overweight defense, energy, and gold. Underweight rate-sensitive sectors. Treasury volatility elevated — monitor basis trade unwind risk. USD strong on safe-haven demand but fiscal trajectory is long-term negative.
narrative.data_quality_note: All key indicators have adequate source coverage. February CPI data is backward-looking and does not capture Iran war oil shock.

name: United States
executive_summary: The US faces a convergence of military, trade, and constitutional crises this week. Operation Epic Fury struck 1,000+ targets in Iran, killing Supreme Leader Khamenei -- the largest US military operation since Iraq 2003. Three US soldiers were killed in IRGC retaliation at Kuwait. The Strait of Hormuz closure sent oil prices surging and Dow futures dropped 517 points. Simultaneously, the SCOTUS struck down IEEPA tariffs 6-3, forcing a pivot to Section 122 with 10-15% global tariffs for 150 days. Additional tariffs on China (20% cumulative) and Canada/Mexico (25%) take effect March 4. Recession probability jumped from 25-30% to 40-50%. The Fed holds at 3.50-3.75% with stagflation risk constraining future rate cuts.
key_changes: US-Israeli strikes hit 1,000+ Iran targets; 3 US soldiers killed in Kuwait; IRGC retaliates against 27 US bases (Feb 28-Mar 1, transformative)
SCOTUS struck down IEEPA tariffs 6-3; Trump pivoted to Section 122 with 15% global tariff (Feb 20, transformative)
Strait of Hormuz effectively closed; oil prices surge to $80-90+ range; gold past $5,300/oz (Mar 1)
Additional 10% China tariff (20% total) and 25% Canada/Mexico tariffs effective March 4
Recession probability surged from 25-30% to 40-50%; S&P 500 posted largest monthly decline in a year
New START treaty expired without extension; global nuclear arms control framework collapsed
outlook: The US faces a potential stagflation scenario: oil price shock from Hormuz disruption pushes inflation higher while recession probability rises on trade disruption and consumer confidence deterioration. The constitutional crisis over trade policy creates legal uncertainty for hundreds of billions in tariff collections, with companies suing for $134B+ in refunds. March FOMC (17-18) is a key decision point -- the Fed faces a dilemma between supporting growth and containing inflation. Trump-Xi summit planned for March 30 could either escalate or de-escalate China trade tensions. Iran military operations continue with no ceasefire in sight, adding persistent defense spending and oil supply risk.
investor_implications: Defensive positioning warranted: overweight Treasuries (10Y at 3.96%), gold ($5,300+), energy, and defense contractors (Lockheed, Raytheon, Northrop). Underweight consumer discretionary, airlines, and trade-exposed manufacturers. The $200B+ tariff refund question creates contingent fiscal liability. Monitor VIX at Monday open and March FOMC for rate path signals. Section 122 tariffs limited to 150 days create a policy cliff in July that will require resolution.

active_alerts.title: US trade policy constitutional crisis: SCOTUS IEEPA ruling, Section 122 imposed
active_alerts.description: SCOTUS struck down IEEPA tariffs 6-3 on Feb 20. Trump imposed Section 122 tariffs (10-15% for 150 days). Additional 10% tariff on China effective March 4. Trade openness trend growth as effective tariff rate dropped from 16% to 9.1%. Companies suing for $134B+ in refunds. Fundamental restructuring of US trade policy legal framework ongoing.

active_alerts.title: US faces inflation uptick from oil shock; political stability decreasing
active_alerts.description: US GDP stable at 2% but inflation trend shifted to growth (2.7%) from oil price surge following Hormuz closure. Debt at 125% with growth trend. Military operations against Iran add defense spending. Political stability trend decrease. Government debt trend growth as SCOTUS ruling eliminates ~$175B in tariff revenue. Fed rate cuts may be delayed.

active_alerts.title: Global nuclear arms control framework collapses
active_alerts.description: New START treaty expired without extension. North Korea tested hypersonic missiles. Russia planning new ICBM tests. Iran nuclear infrastructure targeted in strikes. Global nuclear order in most precarious state since Cold War. Conditions persist.

active_alerts.title: US captures Maduro; oil sanctions eased
active_alerts.description: US captured Maduro Jan 3. OFAC general licenses easing Venezuela oil sanctions. International condemnation. Conditions persist.

active_alerts.title: US-Indonesia sign $33 billion trade agreement
active_alerts.description: US and Indonesia signed a $33 billion trade agreement on Feb 19, significantly strengthening bilateral economic ties. This positions Indonesia as a key US partner in Southeast Asia amid China competition. FDI trend growth. GDP stable at 4.9%. However, SCOTUS IEEPA ruling questions enforceability of executive trade deals.

active_alerts.title: US diplomatic relations strained by Iran strikes and tariff escalation
active_alerts.description: US relations with key partners strained by unilateral Iran strikes. Spain PM condemned strikes as breach of international law. France, Russia, China called UNSC emergency session. Additional 10% tariff on China and 25% on Canada effective March 4. US-Indonesia deal provides counterpoint. Relations trend decrease overall.

economy.sovereign_credit_rating: S&P: AA+, Moody's: Aa1, Fitch: AAA
